content.title: First Read Rule
body: If this is the agent's first time reading this README for the current work mission, read the entire README before opening deeper docs, following local routes, or relying on this corpus.

A first full pass is required once per current work mission, after a context reset, after a handoff or resume, or after the task materially changes. It is not required at every checkpoint in the same active mission.

After the first full pass, use the routing sections below to decide which local docs to reread or inspect next. At later checkpoints, reread only route-relevant sections unless the active `.core.*` route, roadmap, audit, or local stop condition requires a broader reread.
Do not treat this README as authority over App Behavior, architecture, implementation plans, proof, source, repo facts, product decisions, or explicit user instruction.
Living Document
Purpose: Index for detailed large-feature hierarchy examples using Jobs search and Jobs filters as the reference domain.
Audience: Agents and engineers creating complete App Behavior corpuses from feature overview down to acceptance criteria.
Keep In Sync With: Large feature hierarchy model docs and hierarchy item schemas.
Update When: Hierarchy schemas, required fields, or example conventions change.
Does Not Cover: Actual Jobs product authority or implementation status.

content.title: Large Feature Examples
body: This folder shows how to model a large UX feature from high-level overview down to concrete proof rows. The examples are detailed enough to copy as templates.
Files are prefixed in recommended reading order.
1. [Feature Overview](feature-overview.example.json)
2. [Subfeature](subfeature.example.json)
3. [Journey](journey.example.json)
4. [Flow](flow.example.json)
5. [Subflow](subflow.example.json)
6. [Observable Scenario](runtime-scenario.example.json)
7. [Acceptance Criterion](acceptance-criterion.example.json)
8. [Pending Decision](pending-decision.example.json)

content.title: Routing
body: Use this example area when the current task needs a reference for organizing or reasoning about large-feature App Behavior examples.
Does not own target behavior, source authority, implementation details, or proof. Large-feature examples show structure and pattern only.
Stop when the agent can state the large-feature pattern being borrowed, the target artifact it informs, and the target authority that must govern real behavior.
